title: Data Analyst
description:
In het kort
International Card Services (ICS) is een betalingsdienstverlener en marktleider op het gebied van het uitgeven van creditcards in Nederland. We zijn een 100% dochteronderneming van ABN AMRO Bank N.V. De financiële wereld verandert snel. Als toonaangevende speler in deze sector streven we ernaar om innovatie en duurzaamheid te combineren met stabiliteit en veiligheid. Om onze positie verder te versterken en bij te dragen aan onze missie, zijn we op zoek naar een Data Analist die analytisch inzicht en technische expertise samenbrengt om data om te zetten in impactvolle oplossingen voor onze stakeholders binnen ICS.
Je werk
-    Creëren van rapportages en dashboards waarmee o.a. financiële processen, onboarding en klantinteracties gemonitord kunnen worden en relevante KPI’s inzichtelijk worden gemaakt. Je zorgt ervoor dat stakeholders altijd toegang hebben tot actuele en bruikbare informatie.
-    Verzamelen, structureren en analyseren van grote datasets gericht op het verkrijgen van diepgaande inzichten in bijvoorbeeld klantgedrag en operationele efficiëntie, met als doel het verbeteren van processen en klanttevredenheid.
-    Vertalen van complexe data-inzichten naar concrete, begrijpelijke adviezen voor management en stakeholders. Je rapporteert bijvoorbeeld over trends in klantgedrag, financiële- en operationele processen, en geeft aanbevelingen voor optimalisering gebaseerd op jouw analyses.
-    Samenwerken met de andere teams binnen en buiten de Data Office om data-analyses en inzichten te vertalen naar praktische toepassingen binnen projecten gericht op het verbeteren van onboarding, klantbehoud en financiële processen.
-    Uitvoeren van data-validatie en kwaliteitscontroles om ervoor te zorgen dat analyses gebaseerd zijn op nauwkeurige en betrouwbare gegevens.
Werkomgeving
Je komt te werken in het Data Office, het kloppend hart van onze data gedreven transformatie. Vijf teams werken hier dagelijks aan één missie: data inzetten als strategisch vermogen. Het Data Office bestaat onder andere uit een Data Product Management Team, een Data Hub, een AA & AI team en twee Reporting teams. Je zal komen te werken in het Business Reporting team. Dit team is verantwoordelijk voor alle MI (Management Informatie) met betrekking tot Onboarding, Collections, Sales & Marketing, Finance en het Customer Contact Center.
We staan aan het begin van een transitie naar een meer gecentraliseerde en toekomstbestendige dataomgeving. Als data analist speel je een belangrijke rol in het implementeren van gecentraliseerde datamodellen die organisatiebreed gebruikt worden. Daarnaast werk je actief mee aan het rationaliseren en vereenvoudigen van onze bestaande rapportages. Je helpt ons om van versnipperde inzichten naar uniforme en schaalbare informatievoorziening te gaan zodat besluitvorming sneller, consistenter en meer datagedreven wordt.
We zijn een jong en ambitieus team die dagelijks onze business helpt om meer datagedreven te worden en zoeken iemand die het leuk vindt om hier aan bij te dagen.
Je profiel
-    Een afgeronde bachelor- of masteropleiding in een relevant werkgebied. Of een gelijkwaardig denk- en oplossend niveau in een kwantitatief georiënteerd vakgebied.
-    Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in data-analyse binnen de financiële sector, consultancy of een vergelijkbare omgeving. Kennis van de financiële sector is een pré.
-    Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in een data-analistfunctie met PBI (DAX-codering) en SQL.
-    Ruime ervaring met het gebruiken van data-analyse om besluitvorming te beïnvloeden en strategische initiatieven te leiden, waarbij je effectief samenwerkt met stakeholders om inzichten om te zetten in actie..
-    Sterke vaardigheden in stakeholdermanagement, waarbij je proactief en helder communiceert met interne en externe stakeholders om verwachtingen te beheren, draagvlak te creëren en gezamenlijke doelen te bereiken.
-    Ervaring met werken in een scrum/agile-omgeving.
-    Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden in zowel het Nederlands als Engels, in staat om complexe data-gedreven scenario’s duidelijk uit te leggen aan verschillende doelgroepen, inclusief senior management en cross-functionele teams, en om actief input van stakeholders te integreren in je analyses.
-    Toont eigenaarschap door verantwoordelijkheid te nemen voor eigen acties en beslissingen en zet zich in voor het behalen van (afdeling)doelen of het oplossen van problemen.
-    Je bent analytisch sterk, nieuwsgierig en niet bang om de status quo uit te dagen. Je verbindt mensen en data, stelt scherpe vragen en neemt initiatief om verbeteringen te realiseren, waarbij je stakeholders effectief meeneemt in de door jou voorgestelde veranderingen.
Wij bieden
-    Een hybride werkplek (minimaal 2 dagen in de week op ons kantoor in Diemen en de rest vanuit huis).
-    Een maandsalaris variërend van €3.706 tot €5.742 gebaseerd op een 36-urige werkweek, inclusief vakantiegeld en het Benefit budget. (We zoeken iemand met passie, drive en commitment om het verschil te maken. De functie is ingeschaald op niveau 8 tot en met 9, afhankelijk van ervaring, zelfstandigheid en de mate waarin je de brug weet te slaan tussen data, mensen en impact.)
-    Een benefit budget van 11% waarmee je flexibel arbeidsvoorwaarden kunt kopen.
-    Vijf weken vakantie per jaar. Je hebt de mogelijkheid om vier extra weken per jaar bij te kopen.
-    Een persoonlijk ontwikkelbudget van € 1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot  € 3.000.
-    Een jaarkaart voor het openbaar vervoer met gratis reizen door heel Nederland.
-    Een uitstekende pensioenregeling.
Interesse?
Ben je geïnteresseerd? Dan horen we graag van je! Reageer nu op deze vacature door je CV en motivatiebrief toe te voegen. Heb je nog vragen? Dan kan je contact met ons opnemen via lvdsijs@icscards.nl (06 20 66 25 50) of awillering@icscards.nl (06 33 01 10 84). We maken graag kennis met je!
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam

title: Capaciteitsmanager Business Clients & Ecosystems
description:
In het kort
Ben jij die ervaren Capaciteitsmanager die workforce management binnen onze afdeling naar een hoger niveau wil brengen? Ben je een ervaren WFM-er, slim, ambitieus, analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig?  Weet jij als geen ander hoe je interne stakeholders moet overtuigen van jouw adviezen en bevindingen? Dan gaan wij graag met jou in gesprek!
Je werk
Je werkt in een klein WFM team en bent daar verantwoordelijk voor de WFM cyclus en het forecastproces bij Business Clients. Je maakt de forecast voor alle werkstromen die met name via Microsoft Dynamics lopen en onderzoekt hoe we in de toekomst alle werkzaamheden van de business kunnen forcecasten en inplannen via WFM. Je stelt een capaciteitsplan op en geeft het management aan de hand hiervan advies over benodigde capaciteit en werkverdeling. De uitkomsten van het capaciteitsoverleg draag je over aan de Planners en Traffic managers. Je levert een proactieve bijdrage aan de inzet en optimalisatie van WFM. Je rapporteert aan de manager WFM BC&E. Het betreft een fulltime functie.
Werkomgeving
Bij het Centre of Expertise (CoE) WFM zijn alle WFM experts binnen de bank ondergebracht die voor verschillende afdelingen WFM neerzetten en uitvoeren. Je komt te werken in het executie team dat verantwoordelijk is voor WFM bij Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E).
Je standplaats wordt de Wisselwerking 58 in Diemen maar bij het Centre of Expertise WFM werken wij hybride, hierbij bepalen de werkzaamheden wat voor jou die dag de beste werkplek is.
Business Clients & Ecosystems
De client unit Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E) is verantwoordelijk voor het bedienen van zakelijke klanten met een omzet tot 25 miljoen Euro, ingedeeld in 'sectorclusters'. De focus van BC&E ligt op het behalen van onze groeidoelstelling om de nummer één bank voor het midden- en kleinbedrijf  (zakelijke klanten met omzet tot 25 miljoen) te worden.
BC&E bedient +/- 370.000 zakelijke klanten (inclusief Medical Business Clients). De afdeling adviseert zakelijke klanten over dagelijkse zakelijke producten en diensten, zoals leningen tot een miljoen Euro, betaaloplossingen, sparen, verzekeringen, beyond en near banking proposities en ondersteuning bij het dagelijks bankieren.
Je profiel
Meerdere jaren werkervaring op het gebied van Workforce management (WFM); Ervaring met het implementeren van WFM bij een nieuwe afdeling is een pre.
Analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig.
Kennis en ervaring met PowerBI rapportages is een pre.
Wij bieden
Het is voor ons belangrijk om een goede werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden je daarom een functie met impact én uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden die bijdragen aan jouw werkplezier, ontwikkeling en wellbeing.
Daarnaast bieden wij:
Een aantrekkelijk bruto maandsalaris op basis van een 36urige werkweek. Dit is inclusief vakantiegeld en een flexibel benefit budget.
Een uitstekende pensioenregeling, zodat je goed voorbereid bent voor later.
Flexibiliteit in werken: thuiswerken is mogelijk in overleg met je team en afhankelijk van je functie. Wij regelen een ergonomische thuiswerkplek voor je.
Ruimte voor ontspanning met vijf weken vakantie per jaar, aangevuld met twee verplichte vrije dagen. Daarnaast kun je jaarlijks tot vier extra weken vakantie bijkopen.
Vijf ‘Banking for better days’: extra vrije dagen die je kunt inzetten voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling of vrijwilligerswerk.
Persoonlijke ontwikkeling staat centraal: je ontvangt een ontwikkelbudget van €1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot €3.000.
Een OV-jaarabonnement met gratis openbaar vervoer in heel Nederland voor zakelijk en privé gebruik.
Interesse?
Heb je interesse? Reageer dan door middel van een sollicitatie. Voor extra inhoudelijke info graag even reageren naar
thomas.van.hassel@nl.abnamro.com
/ 06 1439 7379.

Banking for better, for generations to come.

persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd
sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
At a glance
Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
Channel Security is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to join our team; Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.
Your job
As a Lead Data Analyst within the Fraud Monitoring team, you will play a central role in ensuring that our analysis and detection logic are grounded in solid reasoning and supported by evidence. You will work closely with Fraud Experts (FEs), Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers to strengthen our fraud detection capabilities across payment channels to catch frauds such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen cards, and other emerging threats.
Your work focuses on deep‑dive analysis, understanding fraud behaviour, evaluating the effectiveness of detection logic, and validating whether proposed changes are analytically sound. You will help the team distinguish clear signals from noise, recognise when evidence is insufficient, and ensure our decisions are based on robust data rather than anecdotal cases.
This is a hands‑on analytical role with significant influence: your insights and your methodological judgment will directly shape how we detect fraud and protect our customers.
Key Responsibilities:
Analytical Excellence
Perform in‑depth analyses to understand fraud patterns, attack vectors, seasonality, behavioural shifts, and detection performance across payment channels.
Evaluate the strength of proposed detection logic (features, thresholds, rules) and identify when ideas are overly narrow, unsupported by evidence, or prone to false positives.
Assess the value and reliability of data fields and signals used in fraud detection.
Help ensure our reasoning, conclusions, and decisions are analytically sound and well‑supported.
Methodological Leadership
Guide Fraud Experts and junior analysts in applying good analytical practices and critical thinking.
Coach colleagues on interpreting data responsibly, avoiding common analytical pitfalls, and understanding basic statistical concepts such as variance, sample bias, and signal strength.
Promote a culture of analytical rigor — ensuring the team adopts more mature, data‑driven ways of working.
Collaboration & Impact
Partner with Data Scientists to transform analytical insights into hypotheses, experiments, and feature ideas for model development.
Act as a bridge between domain experts and technical experts, ensuring clear understanding on both sides.
Identify opportunities to improve how we measure fraud impact, effectiveness of controls, and operational efficiency.
Drive improvements in how we use data in the team — from investigation workflows to decision support tools — focusing on quality, clarity, and insight rather than dashboard volume.
Working environment
Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
Your profile
Qualifications and technical skills:
A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role involving hands-on data exploration, problem solving, and interpretation (e.g., data analyst, fraud analyst, risk analyst, business analyst with strong technical skills, data scientists in analytical track).
Technical skills:
Strong SQL skills for exploratory analysis and investigation.
Hands-on experience in Python for data analysis.
PySpark or experience working with large datasets.
Basic familiarity with visualization tools for communication — not for dashboard engineering.
Ability to frame analytical questions, interpret results correctly, and identify flawed logic or insufficient evidence.
Familiarity with basic statistical concepts (e.g., distributions, variance, seasonality, sampling, simple backtesting principles). Not as a statistician— but as a critical thinker.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
About you
You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.
A Teacher and Mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration
We are offering
Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance;
An industry-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40-hour work week;
A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre-tax fringe benefits;
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year;
An annual public transportation pass;
A solid pension plan.
Interested?
Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
Please note: This vacancy is a repost of a position previously published on 27 November 2025; if you have already received a rejection for this role, reapplying will not be considered.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.

match.llm_rationale: The resume shows strong technical AI/ML skills but lacks direct experience in fraud analysis, financial domains, or data analyst roles, with minimal overlap in key responsibilities like fraud pattern analysis, detection logic evaluation, and methodological leadership required for this senior data analyst position.

title: Senior Data Scientist Finance/Controlling
description:
At a glance
Join our Analytics & Innovation team as a Data Scientist and help Finance Controlling move faster with sharper insights and smarter processes. You’ll lead end-to-end data science projects, sourcing and modelling data in Databricks and building AI/ML solutions from first idea through production. You thrive in a supportive, collaborative team where knowledge sharing is part of the daily rhythm, and you bring strong programming skills (Python, PySpark, SQL), clear communication, and a hands-on, independent mindset. In return, we offer a competitive salary, generous vacation, a personal development budget, and flexible hybrid working options.
Your job
As a Senior Data Scientist, you take a leading role in data science initiatives that contribute to process optimisation and the generation of strategic insights for Finance.  You design, develop and maintain AI and ML models. You initiate innovation projects, make model choices, and are co-responsible for bringing new models into production. Your work sets standards for quality and helps develop the team further.
In your role, you will
Unlock, model and structure internal and external data in our Databricks environment.
Design, develop and maintain advanced models and applications using AI and ML techniques, from experimentation through production deployment.
Perform complex analyses and research, make well-founded model choices, explore new methodologies, and develop and operationalise use cases through to production.
Safeguard the quality of models and analyses, mentor junior data scientists, and promote knowledge sharing and collaboration within the team and stakeholders.
Working environment
Analytics & Innovation is part of Finance Controlling and forms a multidisciplinary, self-organising team. Our mission is to make the Controlling and Finance organisation more data-driven. We focus on three areas:
Data insights: we create new insights for Controllers and the Business they support. We identify new data sources, combine Finance and Business data and apply (advanced) analytics techniques and models. In doing so, we support the transformation from expert-based to model-based decision-making.
Process optimisation: we continuously look for opportunities to improve existing processes, both within Controlling and Finance more broadly. We explore how new technologies such as GenAI/LLMs can support the work of our colleagues.
People & skills: we advise senior management on data opportunities, inspire and inform colleagues about new technologies and contribute to improving analytics skills across the organisation.
Your profile
The ideal candidate holds a master’s degree (WO) and has at least six years of relevant experience. They are skilled in Python, PySpark, SQL, and Databricks, with a proven record of independently initiating and leading complex data science projects. Their strong analytical skills and conceptual thinking enable them to turn complex analyses into actionable insights. With a proactive, solution-focused attitude and a deep sense of responsibility, they are dedicated to continuous learning and staying up to date with the latest developments in data science and technology.
This candidate also dedicates themselves to mentoring junior team members, offering guidance and constructive feedback to support their growth and encourage knowledge sharing within the group. Collaboration is central to their approach, as they actively help build a high-performing team environment and consistently value teamwork.
We are offering
An attractive salary, including holiday allowance and an 11% benefits budget
Five weeks of vacation per year, with the option to purchase additional days
Personal Development Budget of €1,000 per year (up to €3,000 if saved)
Flexible hybrid working (in consultation)
Free nationwide public transport annual pass
Excellent pension scheme
Interested?
We look forward to receiving your application. Want to know more about the role or the team? Don’t hesitate to get in touch, we’re happy to tell you more.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.

External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. No unsolicited services or offers, please.

The location is suitable within the Netherlands, but seniority and specific finance/controlling experience are gaps.

title: Data & AI Performance Manager
description:
In het kort
Do you want to be part of the Data & AI Revolution? Do you have a strong passion for data & AI and do you also believe that these are at the core of unlocking value for customers? Does working in a complex environment energize you? Then come and help us unlock the power of data & AI at the Central Data Office (CDO) of ABN AMRO!
Je werk
You critically support the execution of the ABN AMRO strategy: a Personal Bank in a Digital Age. By monitoring and guiding strategic decisions needed for accelerating execution. You ensure that the entire bank follows the correct route towards a fully data & AI driven organisation. You are energised by submerging yourself in a brand-new initiative, shape, socialise and ensure it is properly anchored for successful execution.
You are responsible for measuring and steering (data & AI) performance across all parties that play a pivotal role. Including Data Domains, CDO, IT, and Executive Board (ExBo). You translate the (bank, data & AI)_ strategies into meaningful performance indicators, provide insight through high‑quality reporting, and enable effective performance dialogues across the organisation.
Key responsibilities include:
Design and operationalise performance managament mechanisms on data & AI across the bank.
Deliver high‑quality management information and ExBo reporting with actionable insights and clear narratives.
Translate strategies into clear, consistent performance indicators (KPIs and metrics) across data domains, CDO, ExBo, and other areas as needed.
Coordinate performance management across stakeholders (data domains, CDO functions, CDO and bank leadership) to ensure consistent objectives, messaging, alignment and ownership.
Identify gaps, inconsistencies, and inefficiencies in end‑to‑end performance measurement and reporting, from data sourcing to reporting.
Propose and drive remediation actions to improve reporting quality, coherence, and actionability of management information.
Facilitate performance dialogues by providing fact‑based insights and highlighting risks, dependencies, and improvement opportunities.
Shape, prioritise, and coordinate the CDO BI dashboard and reporting agenda in close collaboration with the Enterprise Enabling Data Domain BI delivery team.
We are seeking a skilled and experienced individual to join our team, focusing on measuring, steering and optimizing the performance of our data and AI processes. The role operates in a complex stakeholder environment across the bank, requiring strong coordination skills and the ability to connect insights across domains and levels.
Werkomgeving
The team is part of the Data Governance department within the CDO organization. The team consists of 4 employees who directly report to the Head of (Data & AI) Strategy, Design & Performance Management.
Next to your day-to-day work, there is room to design, create and implement new processes especially when you are the one who is suggesting the change for the better! We do so in an open and transparent way of working where your opinion is valued and dialogue is encouraged. The team is diverse with regards to culture, experience and age. The atmosphere is lively and informal, closely monitoring a safe environment where everyone is able to share their thoughts, feelings and worries. It is normal to make mistakes and we aim to help each other grow better as learning is one of our main focuses.
The team you become part of is in constant collaboration with all business lines, international branches and subsidiaries of the bank. We all work together at the frontier of the largest data & AI revolution in the banking industry and with the core value-assets of the organization.
Je profiel
Je profiel
You recognize yourself in the following:
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build and maintain strong relationships across various levels and aligning multiple parties across end‑to‑end processes.
Experience in writing executive-level reporting and performance narrative.
Experience in performance management, KPI design, and management reporting in a complex organisation.
Demonstrated experience with BI delivery, dashboards, and reporting products, including translating business needs into insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex information clearly and persuasively to executive audiences.
Strong analytical capability to identify reporting gaps, root causes, and improvement opportunities.
Proactive, improvement‑driven mindset with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a dynamic environment.
Wij bieden
We believe it is important to be a good employer. Joining us means stepping into a role with impact, supported by excellent employment conditions that foster your job satisfaction, development, and wellbeing.
In addition, we offer:
An attractive gross monthly salary based on a 36hour work week, including holiday allowance and a flexible benefit budget.
An excellent pension scheme, ensuring that you are well prepared for the future.
Flexibility in working: working from home is possible in consultation with your team and depending on your role. We will provide an ergonomic home office setup for you.
Plenty of room for relaxation with five weeks of vacation per year, supplemented by two mandatory days off. You can also purchase up to four additional weeks of vacation annually.
Five “Banking for better days”: extra days off that you can use for personal development or volunteer work.
Personal development is key: you receive a development budget of €1,000 per year, which can accumulate up to €3,000.
An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands for both business and private use.
You can find more information about our employment conditions and salary scales in our Collective Labour Agreement (CLA), which applies to this vacancy.
Interesse?
Are you interested? Please respond to this vacancy before April 20. We would love to meet you!

description:

Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
Why you should join the Unified Platform
The
Unified Platform
powers the core financial infrastructure behind Adyen’s platform - from how transactions are processed to how financial data is stored, monitored, and configured. It plays a critical role in ensuring that payments, accounting, reporting, and reconciliation work seamlessly across all products and customer segments.
We are currently hiring
four Software Engineering Managers
within the Unified Platform. These teams work on different parts of the platform but share a common mission: building scalable, reliable systems that power Adyen’s global financial ecosystem.
Depending on your experience and strengths, you may join one of the following domains:
Transaction Platform
Build and support Adyen’s core transaction platform by providing a highly available API to store and retrieve transaction data and events across the system.
Storage & Scale
Design and evolve the storage architecture behind Adyen’s financial systems, ensuring our data infrastructure can scale reliably as transaction volumes grow.
Finance Observability
Enable transparent and reliable financial operations by building monitoring and alerting systems for critical financial flows across the platform.
Unified Configuration
Create a consistent platform experience by standardizing internal infrastructure and configuration systems that power Adyen’s financial products.
Your final team placement will be determined based on your skills, experience, and the technical fit identified during the interview process.
What you’ll do
Lead, grow, and mentor a team of 5–6 engineers building critical components of Adyen’s financial infrastructure.
Guide the design and development of scalable distributed systems using Core Java, web services, queueing frameworks, REST APIs, and SQL/PostgreSQL.
Collaborate closely with product and platform teams to design systems that are reliable, scalable, and easy to operate.
Balance long-term technical improvements with product delivery.
Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
Set clear team goals aligned with engineering and product strategy.
Improve operational excellence through automation, observability, and system reliability.
Hire and develop engineering talent that thrives in a fast-moving environment.
Who you are
A strong people manager who enjoys coaching engineers and enabling teams to perform at their best.
Technically proficient in backend engineering, with strong experience in Java or other modern programming languages used to build large-scale distributed systems.
Comfortable making difficult decisions and communicating clearly with strong senior engineers.
Skilled at collaborating across teams and aligning stakeholders around technical decisions.
A clear communicator who can explain complex technical concepts to both engineers and non-technical audiences.
Experienced working with senior leadership to shape technical strategy and roadmaps.
Minimum qualifications
3+ years of people management experience
leading engineering teams.
8+ years of experience in backend software engineering.

Machine Learning Scientist
At Adyen, we are the financial technology platform of choice for the world’s leading companies. The Insights team is at the core of this platform, providing the world’s largest merchants with the data and analytics they need to optimize their payment performance. Within this, our Proactive Diagnostics initiative acts as a proactive guard for merchant revenue, closing the loop between detecting an anomaly and providing a clear path to rectification.
We operate at the intersection of Big Data and actionable intelligence. By leveraging Adyen’s global payment flow, we apply advanced statistical models and Causal Inference to not only detect performance drops but to explain the "why" behind them. We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to help us architect the next generation of our diagnostic engine.
In this role, you will:
Build
– Design and scale production-ready ML models to identify anomalies across millions of traffic permutations. You will own the end-to-end lifecycle, from feature engineering within our Big Data ecosystem (Spark/SparkStreaming) to building the internal infrastructure—such as our FastAPI-based labeling service on Kubernetes—needed to generate high-quality ground truth for supervised learning.
Discover
– Move beyond simple detection to build automated root-cause analysis. You will develop logic that translates complex statistical signals into actionable recommendations, helping merchants understand exactly how to optimize their setup.
Scale
– Transition our diagnostic capabilities from daily reporting to near-real-time velocity. You will build the observability layer to track model drift and integrity, ensuring our signals remain the "Gold Standard" for the industry.
Collaborate
– Work at the heart of a product-driven team. You will sit close to our users, gathering continuous feedback to ensure our technical solutions solve real-world business friction and drive product adoption.
Who You Are:
You have 4+ years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or Data Scientist (Anomaly Detection, Time-Series, or Signal Processing).
You have an Engineering-First mindset. You treat ML code like production code and are comfortable managing your own deployments and infrastructure.
You are proficient in Python and Big Data frameworks (PySpark, Airflow, Hadoop, Kafka).
Experience with SparkStreaming/Flink, Docker and Kubernetes is a plus.
You have a strong interest in Causal Inference - you want to prove
why
something happened, not just
that
it happened.
You are a pragmatic problem solver. You prioritize business impact and reliability over model complexity, choosing the right tool for the job to ship solutions that work today.
You are proactively taking the lead in projects, from ideation to deployment. You have experience working with a wide range of stakeholders and can clearly communicate complex outcomes to a wide range of audiences.
You can confidently work in a product team with demanding stakeholders, are able to communicate effectively and have the ability to drive the team’s roadmap, alongside the product and engineering leadership of the team.

title: Machine Learning Manager - GenAI Foundation Models
description: About Us: At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world. Leadership/Team Quote: The team is at the forefront of Generative AI innovation, driving solutions for travel-related chatbots, text generation and summarization applications, Q&A systems, and free-text search. The team you are applying to is spearheading a research effort to develop leading LLMs, specifically designed for the travel domain. This pioneering initiative combines cutting-edge AI research with practical applications, focusing on creating tailored solutions that redefine how travelers plan, book, and experience their journeys. Role Description: In this role, you will lead a team of Machine Learning Scientists and Engineers dedicated to building, training, and deploying cutting-edge Generative AI models. This includes developing foundation models trained on Booking.com’s extensive textual data and creating fine-tuned models designed to tackle complex travel-related tasks. Your leadership will play a pivotal role in advancing the application of AI to transform the travel experience for millions of customers. As a technical manager, you should be passionate about GenAI technology, keep up to date with recent breakthroughs in the field, define and shape the team’s ML roadmap, and not be afraid to get your hands dirty with code when needed. You are expected to be the focal point for all technical aspects, make sure your team members deliver on their tasks, and work together with other stakeholders to define and shape the roadmap of our products. You will work independently and will also be responsible for making technical decisions within your team. When it comes to management, your expertise in handling people will motivate and inspire them to reach outstanding success! You should have experience in developing people. You will mentor and coach your team while working closely with a Product Manager. Key Job Responsibilities and Duties: Leadership in LLM Development- Build, Guide and mentor a team of ML scientists and ML engineers in the development, fine-tuning, and deployment of large language models (LLMs) tailored for the travel domain. Expertise in the engineering aspects of deploying LLMs at scale with minimal latency. This includes optimizing model performance, scalability, and efficiency to meet the demands of real-time, high-traffic applications. Define and communicate the technical vision and strategy for LLM-related initiatives, ensuring alignment with company goals and customer needs. Foster a culture of collaboration, innovation, and excellence within the team. Prioritize work in collaboration with Product Managers, depending on business needs and keeping stakeholders aligned at all times. Translate machine learning vision and strategy into planning and execution, and ensure timely delivery of their plans. Develop innovative ML models, algorithms, and engineering approaches or identify existing ones, with the potential to impact our business. Design and execute applied research plans to understand, apply, test, evolve, and generalize these technologies into reusable frameworks. Translate business problems into viable, reliable and robust ML and AI solutions, accounting for constraints of the production environment. Monitor product health, performance and business impact and act accordingly when requirements are not met. Identify underlying issues and opportunities across domains and situations that are not obviously related through application of structured thinking and logic. Solve issues by applying methods and insights gained from a variety of disciplines, navigating a variety of environments. Make things happen by maintaining motivation and conveying a sense of urgency, focusing on outcomes and accomplishments, while respecting the need to balance long- and short-term goals, by applying influencing techniques and decision making skills. Drive, coach and mentor others through evidence and clear communication, explaining advanced technical concepts in simpler terms. Continuously evolve your craft. Keep up to date with industry and academic standard methodologies, periodically explore new technologies, introduce them to the machine learning community and promote their application in areas where they can generate impact. Actively contribute to Machine Learning at Booking.com through training, exploration of new technologies, interviewing, onboarding and mentoring colleagues. Push for improvements, scaling and extending machine learning tooling and infrastructure, collaborating with central teams. Qualifications & Skills: Leadership Experience: At least 4 years of experience leading ML teams in Natural Language Processing (NLP) or Generative AI (GenAI) domains, with a proven ability to guide teams in achieving impactful results. LLM Expertise: Advanced knowledge and experience in managing teams developing Large Language Models (LLMs), with strong expertise in the engineering aspects of scalable LLM deployment, ensuring optimal performance and minimal latency. Academic and Applied Background: MSc with 6+ years of professional experience, or PhD with 4+ years of experience, applying Machine Learning to solve business challenges. Master’s, PhD, or equivalent experience in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Physics, etc.). Strong advantage for candidates whose MSc or PhD thesis work is related to NLP, showcasing deep research capabilities in this field. End-to-End R&D Proficiency: Demonstrated experience in designing and executing end-to-end research and development plans, from problem definition and data preparation to model deployment and performance monitoring. Proven Impact: A track record of delivering measurable business value through the development and implementation of large-scale machine learning models. Experience on multiple machine learning facets: working with large data sets, model development, statistics, experimentation, data visualization, optimization, software development. Experience collaborating cross functionally in the development of machine learning products (e.g. Developers, UX specialists, Product Managers, etc.). Strong working knowledge of Python, Java, Kafka, Hadoop, SQL, and Spark or similar technologies. Working experience with version control systems. Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal. Leading by example, gaining respect through actions, not your title. Developing your team and motivating them to achieve their goals. Providing feedback timely and managing your key team performance indicators Benefits & Perks - Global Impact, Personal Relevance: Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include: Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave Hybrid working including flexible working arrangements, and up to 20 days per year working from abroad (home country) Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) at Booking.com: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion have been a core part of our company culture since day one. This ongoing journey starts with our very own employees, who represent over 140 nationalities and a wide range of ethnic and social backgrounds, genders and sexual orientations. Take it from our Chief People Officer, Paulo Pisano: “At Booking.com, the diversity of our people doesn’t just build an outstanding workplace, it also creates a better and more inclusive travel experience for everyone. Inclusion is at the heart of everything we do. It’s a place where you can make your mark and have a real impact in travel and tech.” We ensure that colleagues with disabilities are provided the adjustments and tools they need to participate in the job application and interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Application Process: Let’s go places together: How we Hire This role does not come with relocation assistance. Booking.com is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We strive to move well beyond traditional equal opportunity and work to create an environment that allows everyone to thrive. title: Internship | Operational AI and the need for Robust Temporal Small Object Detection AI Techniques
description:
TNO’s Temporal Small Object Detection algorithm offers advanced performance in visual monitoring, providing a reliable solution for real-time surveillance and security applications. Supported by peer-reviewed research and ongoing development, this technology represents a strong step forward in intelligent surveillance. We invite you to join us in contributing to the evolving landscape of smart, automated monitoring systems. Multiple stakeholders in the safety domain are facing a growing challenge: increasing staff shortages that demand smarter and more efficient approaches to surveillance and security operations. In response, these organizations are investing in automation and data driven solutions as part of a broader strategy to strengthen operational effectiveness.
We are advancing surveillance and security systems to strengthen the safety and operational resilience of our partners. A central component of this effort is TNO’s Temporal Small Object Detection AI technology. Unlike conventional single frame detectors, our model incorporates temporal information across video sequences, using motion cues, frame history, and temporal feature learning to substantially improve the detection of very small objects. By leveraging video context, the system delivers increased robustness under challenging conditions such as adverse weather, motion blur, occlusions and rapid appearance changes, where traditional single frame approaches often fall short.
At the same time, we recognize that further development is needed to address current limitations. Ongoing research is therefore focused on closing these gaps, ensuring that the technology becomes fully operational and seamlessly deployable within our partner’s real world environments.
In this internship, you will contribute to the continued advancement of our Temporal Small Object Detection technology, a key component in next generation intelligent surveillance systems. The goal of the assignment is to further strengthen the model’s performance in diverse and dynamic real world conditions. Depending on your interests, you can tailor the focus toward model architecture, training strategies, or data design. Possible directions include exploring novel model architectures that more effectively capture temporal dependencies, experimenting with advanced training techniques, or designing synthetic datasets that represent rare or challenging scenarios. You may also investigate domain adaptation approaches to support reliable performance when deploying the system across different environments. Your work will play a meaningful role in enhancing the reliability and effectiveness of cutting edge surveillance technologies, helping bring research innovations closer to operational deployment.
You’ll carry out this assignment within TNO’s Intelligent Imaging department, located in The Hague. This group of ~60 passionate researchers specializes in AI, Computer Vision and Robotics. The team regularly supervises students on internships and graduation projects, often with overlapping topics that encourage collaboration and knowledge exchange.
You are in the final stages of your master’s degree in artificial intelligence, computer science, mathematics, electrical engineering, robotics, physics or a similar degree. You have some experience in computer vision, artificial intelligence, and programming in Python. In addition to technical experience, we value clear communication, a result-driven attitude, and a motivation to publish the work.

title: Internship | Billing, metering and cost models in federated clouds
description:
Big Tech currently dominates the cloud computing market — TNO is exploring an alternative path. Will you help shape a more open and decentralised cloud landscape by researching billing, metering, and potential cost‑ and collaboration models for federated clouds?
As a Master’s thesis intern, you will take ownership of the research track on billing, metering, and cost/collaboration models for federated clouds. You will work independently to survey the state of the art, extract requirements from real-world use cases, and design and prototype metering and billing mechanisms that function across providers. Expect to model and compare cost- and revenue‑sharing schemes (e.g., clearing/settlement across participants), validate interoperability and portability against open interfaces, and run small-scale experiments on a federated testbed. You’ll engage stakeholders to refine assumptions, translate findings into actionable design choices, and deliver results in a clear thesis and, where possible, reusable artifacts that advance an open cloud ecosystem.
You will join TNO’s ECOFED team—an ambitious, multidisciplinary group building a real federated cloud with European partners. We operate at the forefront of the European cloud ecosystem, turning open, interoperable, and portable‑by‑design ideas into running systems. The team blends cloud engineering, architecture, economics, and standards expertise, offering close guidance while giving you the freedom to lead your work. Our culture is curious, pragmatic, and open‑ecosystem–minded: we collaborate with industry, research, and public-sector stakeholders, value clean interfaces over lock‑in, and measure success by impact on a more open and decentral cloud market.
Master’s-level student in cloud computing, computer science, or a related field.
Strong enthusiasm for cloud technologies and modern cloud architectures.
Motivation to pioneer European digital sovereignty and open cloud ecosystems.
Able to work independently, structure research, and drive progress proactively.
Interest in interoperability, portability, and open standards within multi‑cloud environments.

title: Internship | Enabling Interoperability of Heterogeneous Data Models for Data Sharing in Logistics
description:
The logistics and transport industry operates across multiple modalities—including maritime, air, and road—each governed by its own standards, data models, and regulatory vocabularies at national, European, and international levels. The fragmentation of these creates significant barriers to interoperability, data reuse, and automation across the end-to-end supply chain. By enabling the interoperability (both governance and technical-wise) of heterogeneous standardized data models, your work will help logistics stakeholders—especially SMEs—adopt plug-and-play data sharing interfaces without needing expensive consulting.
You will design and implement syntactic mappings between existing standardized ontologies and data models for an example industry, in this case logistics and transport (L&T). Your work will also include identifying commonalities between the semantics of standardized L&T data models and, together with your supervisor, to define a syntax-free conceptual model of the considered standardized L&T data models.
Where appropriate, you will use state-of-the-art semantic web and knowledge representation approaches, including explicit mapping dictionaries and metadata frameworks (e.g., DCAT, SKOS, controlled vocabularies), to ensure transparency and long-term maintainability.
The ideal candidate for this thesis project is in the final phase of a Master’s in Data Science / Industrial Engineering / Computer Science and has an interest in semantics and data engineering. This candidate enjoys pattern recognition, doesn't shy away from thinking outside the box, and has experience with data engineering, ontology engineering or knowledge representation.
We expect that you can start as soon as possible. The project is designed as a 6 to 9 month full time assignment (with some flexibility), ending no later than December 1st. To support effective collaboration, we expect it is feasible for you to work from the TNO office in Soesterberg or Eindhoven at least twice a week.

title: Internship | AI‑Driven Dataset Curation: Leveraging VLMs to Understand and Improve Dataset Quality
description:
High‑quality datasets are the backbone of trustworthy Artificial Intelligence. Yet, strengthening AI systems through careful dataset curation remains an often underestimated part of model development and deployment. In this internship, you will explore how natural‑language‑driven image quality assessment can enhance dataset curation practices. Where traditional methods rely on numerical indicators such as contrast, noise levels, or resolution, modern multimodal models enable richer, grounded descriptions of image quality. Building on ideas from work like GROUNDING‑IQA: Grounding Multimodal Language Models for Image Quality Assessment, this project investigates how image‑level quality descriptions can be combined into dataset‑level profiles, revealing coverage gaps, biases, and systematic quality inconsistencies. The focus will be on real‑world datasets, with particular attention to how well these quality assessment approaches generalize to niche domains, long‑tail categories, or fine‑grained classes. The results will contribute to more structured, transparent, and explainable dataset curation workflows.
You will design and implement a pipeline that generates and analyzes natural language image quality descriptions, perform dataset-level aggregation and keyword analysis, and evaluate how well these methods generalize across different datasets and domains. Through experiments, you will assess how language-based quality profiling can complement traditional numerical quality metrics in data validation workflows. The precise focus and methods can be tailored to match your interests and strengths.
During your internship, you will write a thesis documenting your research. If the results are promising, we may explore the possibility of a joint publication.
You are in the final stages of your master’s degree in artificial intelligence, computer science, data science, or a related field. You have experience with Python, deep learning, and preferably some familiarity with vision-language models. You are curious about how AI can be made more interpretable and robust in real-world settings.
We expect you to work from our office in The Hague (Oude Waalsdorperweg 63) at least two days per week, but preferably more often. Your supervision team will consist of experienced researchers in computer vision and deep learning, and are actively involved in related projects. Furthermore, they regularly supervise students, often with overlapping topics that encourage collaboration and knowledge exchange.

Whether you are looking for an internship or a graduation project.

title: Internship | On Federated learning platform for Digital twins
description:
Big Data and Data Science are increasingly popular topics because of the advantages they can bring to companies. At TNO we are looking into ways of developing solutions for vertical federated learning framework which allows the separation of concerns between local models, making analysis on local data, and central model which learns from many local models and updates local models when necessary. title: Data Engineer | The Hague
description:
You will work with a team of data engineers on the TNO Data Platform, the foundation for data‑driven decision making within TNO. You make sure we deliver a stable platform with supportive tooling and reliable data- and CI/CD pipelines. You will also collaborate closely with other departments to guarantee effective cooperation and to share knowledge within and beyond the team.
TNO Data & Analytics consists of four teams: data analysts, data scientists, data stewards, and data engineers. You will join the data engineering team, which is currently building a new, TNO‑wide data platform that will serve as the backbone for all our analyses, dashboards, predictive models, and other data products. You will help design modular components and scale this platform for internal users across TNO, ensuring that onboarding new teams is as smooth as possible.
Your responsibilities include onboarding new data sources, automating data transformations (ETL/ELT), building supportive tooling (such as CI/CD and Python packages), implementing data security & governance, and managing cloud infrastructure.
In addition to developing the TNO Data Platform, you will work closely with data analysts, scientists, and stewards within Data & Analytics, as well as with other teams (such as IT), to improve the quality of the data platform and data products, and to build new data‑driven solutions.

As a Network Engineer, your challenge is to manage complex networks that enable over 4,500 TNO employees at 20 locations in the Netherlands to carry out their daily work and research. You have an eye for detail, security, and can perform coordinating tasks.
As a Network Engineer, you are responsible for ensuring the availability and maintenance of the network infrastructure. This includes, among other things, the following tasks and responsibilities:
Ensuring adequate performance and system security.
Conducting performance measurements and trend analyses, and making system adjustments.
Managing and administering changes.
Advising on the urgency, impact, and priority of changes to be implemented.
